Description: Multiple selection column.
A Date/Time column.
A Date/Time column with friendly format.
Date column without time.
Date column without time, with friendly format.
A Person or Group column allowing only Persons.
A Person or Group column allowing only persons, with show profile photos enabled.
A Person or Group Column.
A Person or Group column accepting both Persons and Groups, with show profiles pictures enabled, multi-select.
A Quick Step column calling a quick step that sends an email.
A number column with default settings, automatic number of decimal places.
A number column with default format and no decimal places allowed.
A number column with dollar sign and 2 fixed decimals.
A currency column with automatic decimal places and USD format.
A yes/no column
A location column with several options enabled.
A Location column with no options enabled.
An image column.
A hyperlink column.
A single-select managed metadata column.
A single-select managed metadata column displaying full item path (item plus parents).
A multi-select managed metadata column.
A single-select lookup column, pointing to People list and pulling the default column (Title, renamed Full Name).
A multi-select lookup column, pointing to People list and pulling the default column (Title, renamed Full Name).

A multi-select lookup column, pointing to People list and pulling a non-default column (UniqName instead of title), plus one additional field (PreferredEmail), and one additional calculated field (Email).
Custom tags and keywords for searching.

A test field whose names starts with an underscore, which is typically hidden. In APIs it is usually presented as "OData_"+field name. Example: _CustomSystemColumn -> OData__CustomSystemColumn (notice the double underscore).
A calculated column returning the text, "Hello World".
A calculated column returning a Yes/No (Boolean) value, to show how it may differ in comparison to regular Yes/No columns.
